---
title: "User Skills"
---

## Machine Learning with Tidymodels

These skills help you build and evaluate predictive models using Tidymodels for data analysis and machine learning tasks.

## Installation

To use user skills, first add the Tidymodels skills marketplace, then install the user skills collection:

```claude
/plugin marketplace add tidymodels/skills
/plugin install tidymodels-users@tidymodels-skills
```

See the [Getting Started Guide](../getting-started.qmd) for Claude Code installation and detailed setup instructions.

## Available Skills

<div class="skill-section">
  <img src="../assets/logos/tidymodels.png" class="skill-logo-inline">
  <h3><a href="tabular-data-ml/index.qmd">Tabular Data Machine Learning</a></h3>
</div>

Build predictive models for tabular data with proper validation practices.

**What you'll learn:**

- **Data Spending**: Proper train/test splitting with strict test set protection
- **Empirical Validation**: Cross-validation and resampling strategies
- **Performance Metrics**: Classification (ROC-AUC, Brier score) and regression (RMSE, R²)
- **Feature Engineering**: Preprocessing and transformation techniques
- **Model Tuning**: Hyperparameter optimization with Tidymodels
- **Model Evaluation**: Visualization and final test set evaluation

**Key principle**: All model development happens on training data using out-of-sample validation. Test set evaluation only occurs with explicit user permission.

---

<div class="skill-section">
  <img src="../assets/logos/tidymodels.png" class="skill-logo-inline">
  <h3><a href="time-series-forecasting/index.qmd">Time Series Forecasting</a></h3>
</div>

Forecast time series data using the modeltime ecosystem with proper temporal validation.

**What you'll learn:**

- **Temporal Data Splitting**: Time-aware splits that respect chronological ordering
- **Backtesting**: Time series cross-validation (not random CV) for honest estimates
- **Model Variety**: Classical (ARIMA, ETS, Prophet) and ML (XGBoost, Random Forest) approaches
- **Feature Engineering**: Creating date-based features for machine learning models
- **Ensemble Methods**: Combining model strengths for improved forecasts
- **Test Set Protection**: Final evaluation only with explicit user permission

**Key principle**: All model development uses time series cross-validation on training data. Random cross-validation is never used. Test set evaluation only occurs with explicit user permission.

---

## Prerequisites

Before using user skills, ensure you have:

- R 4.1.0 or higher
- tidymodels packages: `install.packages("tidymodels")`

See the [Getting Started Guide](../getting-started.qmd) for detailed setup instructions.

## Workflow

1. **Load the skill** when starting a modeling project
2. **Describe your data and goal** - be specific about prediction task
3. **Iterate with Claude Code** through data splitting, feature engineering, and model development
4. **Review visualizations and metrics** from cross-validation
5. **Finalize model** and evaluate on test set (with permission)

## Get Involved

Interested in contributing more user skills? Explore [Developer Skills](../developers/index.qmd) to create Tidymodels extensions.
